value.author: Guerdjikova AI
value.subject: Long-term BED Review
value.summary: Review confirming LDX superiority in binge suppression.

value.citation: Pliszka S. "Practice Parameter for the Assessment and Treatment of ADHD." 2007;46(7):894-921.
value.summary: Establishes "Switching Classes" protocol: if one stimulant class fails, the other must be tried.

value.citation: Wolraich ML, et al. "Clinical Practice Guideline for ADHD in Children/Adolescents." 2019;144(4).
value.summary: Mandates titration to "maximal clinical benefit" and individualized dosing regardless of age/weight.

value.citation: Faraone SV, et al. "The International Consensus Statement on ADHD." 2021;20(3).
value.summary: Review of 208 reports proving untreated ADHD causes higher mortality and long-term morbidity.

value.citation: "Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder: Diagnosis and Management." (Guideline NG87). 2018.
value.summary: International standard confirming long-acting stimulants as primary pharmacological intervention.

value.citation: Ermer JC, et al. "Lisdexamfetamine Dimesylate: Prodrug Delivery, Exposure and Efficacy." 2016;36(5).
value.summary: Documents the 13–14 hourduration of action and the unique blood-based enzymatic conversion.

value.citation: Ermer JC, et al. "Pharmacokinetics of LDX in Healthy Adult Subjects." 2012;40(2).
value.summary: Proves that gastric pH and food intake do not alter the drug’s absorption or efficacy.

value.citation: Childress AC, et al. "Update on PK Considerations in ADHD Treatment." 2019;15(11):937-974.
value.summary: Compares delivery systems; justifies why generic "equivalents" fail due to different release curves.

value.citation: Boellner SW, et al. "Pharmacokinetics of LDX in Water and Orange Juice." 2009;31(10).
value.summary: Confirmed stability in liquids; essential for justifying use in patients with dysphagia (swallowing issues).

value.citation: Heal DJ, et al. "Abuse Liability and the Prodrug Mechanism of LDX." 2013;64:491-501.
value.summary: Molecular proof that LDX is inactive until converted in the blood, preventing abuse via snorting.

value.citation: Jasinski DR, et al. "Human Abuse Potential of Intravenous LDX." 2007;27(1).
value.summary: Clinical data showing "Drug Liking" scores for LDX were statistically similar to placebo.

value.citation: Setlik J, et al. "Analysis of Poison Control Data: LDX vs. Mixed Salts." 2014;141.
value.summary: Real-world data showing significantly lower intentional misuse reports for Vyvanse.

value.citation: Kollins SH, et al. "Pharmacokinetics and Abuse Liability of LDX." 2012;73(11).
value.summary: Proves the "lag time" in dopamine release makes the drug less reinforcing than IR salts.

value.citation: Cortese S, et al. "Comparative Efficacy and Tolerability: A Meta-Analysis." 2018;5(9).
value.summary: Analysis of 133 trials—the definitive proof of stimulant efficacy across the lifespan.

value.citation: Castells X, et al. "Amphetamines for ADHD in Adults." 2018;8(8).
value.summary: Systematic review confirming amphetamines as the most effective adult treatment.

value.citation: Adler LA, et al. "Long-term Safety and Effectiveness of LDX in Adults." 2017;21(2).
value.summary: 12-month data confirming stability and continued safety in adult populations.

value.citation: Frampton JE. "Lisdexamfetamine: A Review in ADHD in Adults." 2016;30(4):343-355.
value.summary: Comprehensive review justifying LDX for adults failing other medications.

value.citation: Fornaro M, et al. "Systematic Review and Meta-analysis of LDX in BED." 2016;12:28062.
value.summary: Confirmed BED remission rates and global improvement over placebo.

value.citation: "Reduction in Weekly Incidence of Binge Eating with LDX 70mg." 2022.
value.summary: Evidence-based review of the 70mg dose as the clinical standard for BED.

value.citation: Brown TE, et al. "Management of ADHD and Executive Functioning." 2020;22(3).
value.summary: Links LDX directly to the improvement of "Self-Management" skills.

value.citation: Doshi JA, et al. "Economic Impact of Step Therapy in ADHD." 2015;18(3).
value.summary: Proves that restrictive insurance policies (Step Therapy) increase total costs.

value.citation: Raees M, et al. "Indirect Costs and Workplace Accidents in ADHD." 2013;19(1).
value.summary: Shows effective treatment reduces workplace liability and emergency room visits.

value.citation: Goodman DW, et al. "Optimizing ADHD Treatment Dosing." 2015;20(1).
value.summary: Argues against "cookie-cutter" dosing; justifies medical necessity for higher/lower doses.

value.citation: Coghill D, et al. "Treatment of ADHD and Comorbid Disorders." 2014;171(2).
value.summary: Shows that treating ADHD reduces the symptoms of ODD and social impairments.

value.citation: Sobanski E, et al. "Emotional Lability and Stimulants in ADHD." 2015;230(2).
value.summary: Specifically looks at "meltdowns" and emotional regulation in treated patients.

value.citation: Faraone SV. "The Biology of ADHD and the Mechanism of Stimulants." 2012;9(3).
value.summary: Explains why dopamine transporters are the primary target for effective therapy.

value.citation: Kay B, et al. "Stimulant Medications Act on Reward and Wakefulness Centers." 2025;188.
value.summary: Latest 2025 research showing stimulants mimic "good sleep" patterns in the brain.

value.citation: DuPaul G, et al. "College Students with ADHD: Treatment and Outcomes." 2012;2(4).
value.summary: Direct correlation between LDX use and GPA/academic organization in young adults.

value.citation: Schoenfelder E, et al. "Adherence and Persistence with ADHD Medications." 2019;14(10).
value.summary: Shows patients stay on Vyvanse significantly longer than they do on generic salts.

value.citation: Cortese S, et al. "Twenty Years of Research on ADHD: Looking Back/Forward." 2018;21(4).
value.summary: Review of two decades of safety data; rebuts claims of long-term neurotoxicity.

value.citation: Caye A, et al. "Transitioning ADHD Care from Pediatrics to Adult Medicine." 2014;5:11-19.
value.summary: Clinical guidelines for maintaining pharmacological stability during age transitions.

value.citation: Cortese S. "Pharmacologic Treatment of ADHD and Cardiac Risk." 2020;383(11).
value.summary: Large-scale review showing no increased risk of major adverse cardiac events in healthy patients.

value.citation: Ermer J, et al. "PK Profile of LDX in Japanese and Caucasian Subjects." 2020;40(1).
value.summary: Proves consistency of drug delivery across different ethnic and metabolic groups.

value.citation: Caye A, et al. "Treatment Strategies: Evidence-Based Selection." 2019;24(3).
value.summary: Guide to selecting stimulants based on the individual patient's symptom profile.
